# terrible_community_vmware_vmware_cluster_ha (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cluster_name` (String) The name of the cluster to be managed.
- `datacenter` (String) The name of the datacenter.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `advanced_settings` (String) A dictionary of advanced HA settings.
- `apd_delay` (Number) The response recovery delay time in sec for storage failures categorized as All Paths Down (APD). Only set if `apd_response=restartConservative` or `apd_response=restartAggressive`.
- `apd_reaction` (String) VM response recovery reaction for storage failures categorized as All Paths Down (APD). Only set if `apd_response=restartConservative` or `apd_response=restartAggressive`.
- `apd_response` (String) VM storage protection setting for storage failures categorized as All Paths Down (APD).
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `enable` (Boolean) Whether to enable HA.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `failover_host_admission_control` (String) Configure dedicated failover hosts. `slot_based_admission_control`, `reservation_based_admission_control` and `failover_host_admission_control` are mutually exclusive.
- `ha_host_monitoring` (String) Whether HA restarts virtual machines after a host fails. If set to `enabled`, HA restarts virtual machines after a host fails. If set to `disabled`, HA does not restart virtual machines after a host fails. If `enable=false`, this value is ignored.
- `ha_restart_priority` (String) Priority HA gives to a virtual machine if sufficient capacity is not available to power on all failed virtual machines. Valid only when `ha_vm_monitoring=vmAndAppMonitoring` or `ha_vm_monitoring=vmMonitoringOnly`. If set to `disabled`, then HA is disabled for this virtual machine. If set to `high`, then virtual machine with this priority have a higher chance of powering on after a failure, when there is insufficient capacity on hosts to meet all virtual machine needs. If set to `medium`, then virtual machine with this priority have an intermediate chance of powering on after a failure, when there is insufficient capacity on hosts to meet all virtual machine needs. If set to `low`, then virtual machine with this priority have a lower chance of powering on after a failure, when there is insufficient capacity on hosts to meet all virtual machine needs.
- `ha_vm_failure_interval` (Number) The number of seconds after which virtual machine is declared as failed if no heartbeat has been received. This setting is only valid if `ha_vm_monitoring=vmAndAppMonitoring` or `ha_vm_monitoring=vmMonitoringOnly`. Unit is seconds.
- `ha_vm_max_failure_window` (Number) The number of seconds for the window during which up to `ha_vm_max_failures` resets can occur before automated responses stop. Valid only when `ha_vm_monitoring=vmAndAppMonitoring` or `ha_vm_monitoring=vmMonitoringOnly`. Unit is seconds. Default specifies no failure window.
- `ha_vm_max_failures` (Number) Maximum number of failures and automated resets allowed during the time that `ha_vm_max_failure_window` specifies. Valid only when `ha_vm_monitoring=vmAndAppMonitoring` or `ha_vm_monitoring=vmMonitoringOnly`.
- `ha_vm_min_up_time` (Number) The number of seconds for the virtual machine's heartbeats to stabilize after the virtual machine has been powered on. Valid only when `ha_vm_monitoring=vmAndAppMonitoring` or `ha_vm_monitoring=vmMonitoringOnly`. Unit is seconds.
- `ha_vm_monitoring` (String) State of virtual machine health monitoring service. If set to `vmAndAppMonitoring`, HA response to both virtual machine and application heartbeat failure. If set to `vmMonitoringDisabled`, virtual machine health monitoring is disabled. If set to `vmMonitoringOnly`, HA response to virtual machine heartbeat failure. If `enable=false`, then this value is ignored.
- `host_isolation_response` (String) Indicates whether or VMs should be powered off if a host determines that it is isolated from the rest of the compute resource. If set to `none`, do not power off VMs in the event of a host network isolation. If set to `powerOff`, power off VMs in the event of a host network isolation. If set to `shutdown`, shut down VMs guest operating system in the event of a host network isolation.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `pdl_response` (String) VM storage protection setting for storage failures categorized as Permenant Device Loss (PDL).
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `reservation_based_admission_control` (String) Configure reservation based admission control policy. `slot_based_admission_control`, `reservation_based_admission_control` and `failover_host_admission_control` are mutually exclusive.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `slot_based_admission_control` (String) Configure slot based admission control policy. `slot_based_admission_control`, `reservation_based_admission_control` and `failover_host_admission_control` are mutually exclusive.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
